---
title: "Jiho Min"
description: "Building useful tools with AI, code, and curiosity."
image: profile.png
---

::: {.site-shell}
::: {.hero-dashboard}
::: {.hero-content}
::: {.eyebrow}
Student developer · AI tools · Web projects
:::

# Building useful tools with AI, code, and curiosity.

I’m a student developer interested in AI tools, web development, productivity systems, finance, and practical software projects. This site is my personal hub for projects, notes, learning, and experiments.

::: {.hero-actions}
[View Projects](projects.qmd){.primary-action}
[Read Notes](notes.qmd){.secondary-action}
[Contact](contact.qmd){.secondary-action}
:::
:::

::: {.hero-panel}
::: {.status-card .accent-card}
**Current focus**

Redesigning this personal website and organizing a clearer project archive.
:::

::: {.mini-grid}
::: {.metric-card}
**Mode**

Learning by building
:::

::: {.metric-card}
**Interests**

AI · Web · Finance
:::
:::
:::
:::

::: {.section-heading #featured-projects}
## Featured projects

A few practical tools and experiments that represent what I like building: focused, useful, and easy to improve.
:::

::: {.cards-grid}
::: {.project-card}
::: {.card-topline}
<span>Education</span><span>In progress</span>
:::

### Social Study App

A quiz-based study web app for middle school social studies.

**Problem it solves:** Makes review sessions more active and easier to repeat before tests.

**Stack:** React, TypeScript, local data, responsive UI

::: {.card-actions}
[Live Demo](#){.project-link}
[GitHub](#){.project-link}
:::
:::

::: {.project-card}
::: {.card-topline}
<span>Productivity</span><span>Prototype</span>
:::

### Reading Room Seat Tracker

A lightweight tool for checking and organizing reading room seat availability.

**Problem it solves:** Reduces confusion around open seats and study space planning.

**Stack:** HTML, CSS, JavaScript, simple data model

::: {.card-actions}
[Live Demo](#){.project-link}
[GitHub](#){.project-link}
:::
:::

::: {.project-card}
::: {.card-topline}
<span>AI</span><span>Experiments</span>
:::

### AI Study Tools

Experiments using AI to improve studying, memorization, and productivity.

**Problem it solves:** Turns repetitive study tasks into guided workflows and review prompts.

**Stack:** AI workflows, prompt design, web prototypes

::: {.card-actions}
[Live Demo](#){.project-link}
[GitHub](#){.project-link}
:::
:::
:::

::: {.section-heading}
## Recent notes

Short writing about development, studying, tools, and systems I am exploring.
:::

::: {.notes-grid}
::: {.note-card}
<span class="note-category">Devlog</span>
### Designing a maintainable personal hub

<span class="note-date">2026-07-05</span>

How I think about turning a portfolio into a useful home base instead of a static resume.

[Read more](notes.qmd){.text-link}
:::

::: {.note-card}
<span class="note-category">AI Tools</span>
### Using AI as a study assistant

<span class="note-date">2026-07-05</span>

A practical note on prompts, review loops, and checking AI output while studying.

[Read more](notes.qmd){.text-link}
:::
:::

::: {.split-section}
::: {.panel-card}
## Now

- Redesigning my personal website
- Building small web tools
- Studying programming and AI workflows
- Organizing projects and notes
- Improving productivity systems

[See current focus](now.qmd){.text-link}
:::

::: {.panel-card}
## Contact & links

The best way to reach me is by email or GitHub. I keep public links simple and avoid private information here.

::: {.link-list}
[Email](mailto:hello@example.com)
[GitHub](https://github.com/jiho-m0521)
[Website](https://mjiho.com)
:::
:::
:::
:::
